title: [firtool] Memories are compiled with randomization logic, but not macros

features.name: memory module initialization with external macro dependencies
features.description: Code should include a memory module with an initial block that uses randomization macros (INIT_RANDOM_PROLOG_, RANDOMIZE_MEM_INIT) but does not define them locally

features.name: conditional memory initialization with RANDOMIZE_MEM_INIT
features.description: Code should include a memory initialization block guarded by RANDOMIZE_MEM_INIT preprocessing directive inside an initial block

features.name: INIT_RANDOM_PROLOG_ macro usage in module scope
features.description: Code should include a module-level initial block that references INIT_RANDOM_PROLOG_ macro without local definition
features.snippet:
`ifdef ENABLE_INITIAL_MEM_
  reg [31:0] _RANDOM_MEM;
  initial begin
    `INIT_RANDOM_PROLOG_
    `ifdef RANDOMIZE_MEM_INIT
      Memory[0] = `RANDOM;
    `endif
  end
`endif

features.name: memory initialization loop with RANDOM macro
features.description: Code should include a for loop inside an initial block that assigns random values to consecutive memory locations using the RANDOM macro
